title: Alteration of the subducting oceanic lithosphere at the southern central Chile trench–outer rise
abstract: New geophysical evidence documents changes in the seismic structure of the incoming oceanic Nazca plate from the outer rise to just before its subduction at ~43°S in the deep-sea trench. Fast Pn velocities of >8.3 km/s were detected more than 200 km seaward of the outer rise, reflecting dry-peridotite composition. Towards the trench, uppermost mantle velocities become systematically slower (~7.8 km/s), interpreted as progressive serpentinization through bending-related fracturing.

location.description: Incoming Nazca plate at ~43°S, outer rise and trench area offshore southern Chile (Chiloé offshore)

seismic_lines.name: TIPTEQ Profile P05 — OBS wide-angle refraction (~43°S, 280 km from Chile Rise)
TIPTEQ SCS01 — coincident high-res reflection line (~43°S)

data.name: SPOC/TIPTEQ Wide-Angle OBS Refraction Data — Outer Rise ~43°S
data.description: Wide-angle P-wave refraction data from two profiles across the incoming Nazca plate at ~43°S documenting velocity changes from dry peridotite (>8.3 km/s) to serpentinized mantle (~7.8 km/s) approaching the trench. [SPOC cruise (SO161, 2001) datasets searchable on PANGAEA]

data.name: Swath Bathymetry — South Chile Outer Rise
data.description: Multibeam bathymetry from SPOC cruise used to characterize outer rise bending-related faulting and topography. [SPOC cruise (SO161, 2001) datasets searchable on PANGAEA]

data.name: Shipboard Gravity — South Chile Outer Rise
data.description: Free-air gravity data from SPOC/TIPTEQ cruises used to constrain crustal thickness at the outer rise

_data_note: SPOC (SO161) and TIPTEQ (SO181) data. OBS data accessible via MGDS. Open access PDF confirmed via Semantic Scholar.

processing.summary: Wide-angle P-wave seismic refraction data from two profiles across the incoming Nazca plate at ~43°S (from SPOC SO161 and TIPTEQ SO181 cruises) were processed using forward and inverse travel-time modeling to derive 2D Vp models. Progressive velocity reduction from >8.3 km/s far seaward of the outer rise to ~7.8 km/s at the trench axis documents systematic serpentinization of the uppermost oceanic mantle by bending-related fracturing.
workflow: 2. Instrument relocation using direct water wave arrivals
processing.notes: Two profiles reported; Vp models derived from OBS travel-time inversion document serpentinization gradient in uppermost mantle

analysis_notes: Validated against PDF. Author corrected: Bialas removed, Heesemann (Martin, Univ. Bremen) added. Vessel corrected to SO181 TIPTEQ (Dec 2004–Feb 2005); year_acquired 2001→2004. Profile name corrected to P05 (+ coincident SCS01). source_volume_cui=3906 (8×8 L G-guns = 64 L for wide-angle). SCS: 100m 16-ch streamer, 1.5 L GI gun. RAYINVR removed (paper uses Korenaga et al. 2000 = TOMO2D). 38 OBH/OBS deployed at 5.5 km spacing.
